mechanism_narrative: A higher 3-month growth rate in INDPRO signals accelerating real output and improving operating leverage, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month INDPRO growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or has negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply, household income risk and recession probability increase, causing equity investors to cut earnings multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its 3-month prior level by more than 0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate combined with decelerating CPIAUCSL lowers discount rates without creating an inflation scare, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change < 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth below its 12-month growth rate
failure_condition: Reject if the joint easing-disinflation trigger is not followed by above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of out-of-sample cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous gains in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicate that consumer spending is supported by job growth, causing upward revisions to revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive RSAFS and PAYEMS condition produces 20-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional mean over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in HOUST signals weakening housing investment and tighter household balance-sheet conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses more than 10% below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakdown triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if downside hit rate is not above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS moves into a restrictive range, higher discount rates and tighter credit conditions reduce equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS-above-4.5% triggers have average 20-day S&P 500 returns equal to or above the unconditional average in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: An upside acceleration in CPIAUCSL raises expected policy tightening and compresses valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses more than 1 percentage point above its 12-month growth rate
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration triggers are followed by nonnegative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of cases or if the average triggered return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with flat or falling PAYEMS signals a productivity-led rebound that lifts output before labor costs rise, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the productivity-rebound condition has negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or a positive-return hit rate below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
